title: quelchia bracteata
intro: Quelchia bracteata, also known as the False Yerba Santa, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its fragrant leaves and white to purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Quelchia bracteata include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and alleviating symptoms of allergies and asthma. Herbal preparations of Quelchia bracteata often include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to soothe skin conditions, relieve congestion, and promote overall well-being.
